task_completion.description: Open the steel microstructure image in Fiji/ImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji/ImageJ interface is visible with the image 'steel_microstructure.jpg' loaded in an image window.
The image window title bar or Fiji status bar shows '8-bit', or the Image > Type menu shows '8-bit' being selected.

task_completion.description: Apply a median filter with a 2.0-pixel radius.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Median' filter dialog box is visible with 'Radius' set to 2.0 pixels, or the image appears slightly smoothed/blurred compared to the original.

task_completion.description: Segment the Pearlite phase using the Otsu auto-threshold method for dark regions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Threshold' dialog is open with 'Otsu' selected in the dropdown. The dark regions (Pearlite) should be highlighted in red or shown as foreground in a binary image.

task_completion.description: Measure the Area Fraction of the segmented Pearlite phase.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Results' window in Fiji is visible and contains a measurement row with an '%Area' or 'Area Fraction' column populated with a numerical value.

task_completion.description: Save the binary segmentation mask and measurement results table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File save dialogs, Fiji macro code, or terminal commands showing 'pearlite_mask.png' and 'phase_measurements.csv' being saved to the '~/Fiji_Data/results/metallurgy/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a text report containing the Pearlite fraction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the creation of '~/Fiji_Data/results/metallurgy/report.txt' containing exactly one line in the format 'Pearlite_Fraction: XX.X' (where XX.X is the measured value).

integrity.description: The agent used the Fiji/ImageJ application to perform the image processing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Fiji/ImageJ GUI being interacted with (menus, dialogs, image windows), rather than just running a pre-written Python/bash script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The reported Pearlite fraction comes from an actual measurement in Fiji.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical value entered in 'report.txt' matches the '%Area' or 'Area Fraction' value seen in the Fiji 'Results' window.

integrity.description: The mask and CSV files were genuinely generated by Fiji.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses Fiji's 'Save As' functionality (or a Fiji macro) to export the .png mask and .csv results, rather than mocking the files manually in a terminal or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires processing 'steel_microstructure.jpg' using Fiji/ImageJ. The exact pipeline is: Convert to 8-bit grayscale -> Apply Median filter (radius 2.0) -> Apply Otsu auto-threshold (selecting dark regions for Pearlite) -> Measure Area Fraction. The final outputs must be saved to ~/Fiji_Data/results/metallurgy/ as pearlite_mask.png, phase_measurements.csv, and report.txt (containing exactly 'Pearlite_Fraction: XX.X').
